project.title: The E3 ubiquitin ligase FBXL18 stabilizes BST2 to promote inflammation in RABV-infected astrocytes
project.projectDescription: we  identify the ubiquitnation sites of BST2 and used quantitative proteomic analysis to compare protein abundance between WT MA-C cells and those that have received FBXL18-specific siRNAs
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: For in-gel protein digestion, gel bands were incubated with 10mM dithiothreitol at 56 °C for30min, and alkylated with 55mM Chloroacetamide at roomtemperature for 20min in darkness. Samples were digested by trypsin. LC-MS analyses of peptide samples were carried out on a Orbitrap mass spectrometer (Thermo Scientific) in a data-dependent acquisition mode.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: MS/MS spectra were searched against Mus_musculus_10090_SP_20220107 and BST2 protein database using Maxquant and Protein Discover software. Key search parameters included mass tolerance of 20 ppm for precursor ions and 0.02 Da for MS/MS ions, a maximum of two missed cleavages allowed, carbamidomethylation of Cys (+57.02146 Da) as static modification,Lys diGly (+114.042927 Da) and  Met oxidation (+15.99492 Da) as dynamic modification.
